question_text: Two children push a toy box across the floor at a constant velocity. One child pushes with a force of 25 N to the right. Friction acts with a force of 15 N to the left. What force must the second child apply to keep the box moving at constant velocity?
explanation: For constant velocity, forces must be balanced. The total rightward force must equal the leftward friction force of 15 N. Since one child pushes with 25 N to the right, the second child must push 10 N to the right, making the total 25 N, which balances the 15 N friction.
